name: King's Gambit
description: A sharp gambit where White sacrifices the f-pawn to open lines and seize initiative.

name: King's Gambit Accepted
description: Black accepts the gambit pawn and enters open, tactical play.

name: Vienna Gambit
description: An attacking Vienna branch where White plays f4 and aims for fast initiative.

name: Center Game
description: A direct central opening where White immediately challenges Black's center with d4.

name: Sicilian Defense Rossolimo Variation
description: A practical anti-Sicilian where White uses Bb5 to avoid heavy theory and apply pressure.

name: Sicilian Defense Moscow Variation
description: A Bb5+ anti-Sicilian system that aims to sidestep mainline theory and keep structure flexible.

name: Sicilian Defense Accelerated Dragon
description: A faster Dragon setup where Black aims for ...g6 without committing ...d6 too early.
movesPreview: 1. e4 c5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. d4 cxd4 4. Nxd4 g6

name: Sicilian Defense Taimanov Variation
description: A flexible Sicilian system built around ...e6 and ...Nc6 with many move-order options.
movesPreview: 1. e4 c5 2. Nf3 e6 3. d4 cxd4 4. Nxd4 Nc6

name: Tarrasch Defense
description: A Queen's Gambit defense where Black accepts an isolated queen's pawn for active piece play.

name: Chigorin Defense
description: An unusual Queen's Gambit defense where Black develops the knight to c6 instead of supporting with e6 or c6.

name: Budapest Gambit
description: A surprise weapon where Black gambits a pawn early for activity and tactical chances.

name: Old Indian Defense
description: A compact Indian defense where Black develops classically and avoids heavy theoretical battles.

name: Réti Gambit
description: A flank-opening idea where White can offer a pawn for quick activity and central pressure.

name: Polish Opening
description: An offbeat opening starting with b4, aiming for queenside expansion and unusual structures.
